meta_title: For Students: Claude Code skills setup to Solve no version control (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to Claude Code skills setup and overcome no version control. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Claude Code Setup to Solve Version Control
content.seo_description: Master version control and accelerate your development workflow. Learn to configure Claude Code and Antigravity for seamless, AI-assisted project management.
content.answer_block: To eliminate version control chaos, integrate Claude Code with Antigravity to automate repository snapshots and branch management. By defining AI-agent permissions and structured commit workflows within your CLI, you ensure every code iteration is tracked, documented, and recoverable, transforming your messy experimental build into a professional-grade software portfolio.
content.intro:
You are staring at a folder of 'final_v2_real_final' files, terrified that your next line of code might break the only working version you have. This isn't just a lack of organization; it is a bottleneck that kills your productivity and keeps you from shipping real-world applications. When your development process lacks rigorous version control, your portfolio suffers, and your confidence withers.

But what if your development environment could manage itself? By leveraging Claude Code alongside Antigravity, you can offload the cognitive burden of state management to an intelligent agent. This setup doesn't just save your work—it transforms your terminal into a command center that treats every experimental branch as a production-ready feature.

steps.title: Initialize Repository and Environment
steps.description: Establish a clean state by initializing Git and linking your local directory to a controlled environment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this directory structure and generate a comprehensive .gitignore file for a Node.js/TypeScript project to prevent tracking unnecessary build artifacts.
steps.claude_code_command: git init && claude config set --autosave true

steps.title: Configure Claude Code Agentic Permissions
steps.description: Grant Claude Code the necessary read-write access to manage your repository branches and commit history.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a system instruction for my Claude Code agent that forces it to explain every file change before executing a commit.

steps.title: Automate Branching Strategy
steps.description: Use AI to enforce a feature-branch workflow, ensuring your main branch remains stable at all times.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a script that checks out a new branch named 'feat/description' before allowing Claude to modify core logic.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a new feature branch for the authentication module'

steps.title: Implement AI-Assisted Semantic Commits
steps.description: Ensure every commit follows the Conventional Commits specification for maximum clarity and traceability.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current diff and generate a Conventional Commit message that strictly follows the 'type: description' format.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Commit these changes with a descriptive conventional commit message'

steps.title: Validate Integrity with Agentic Reviews
steps.description: Use Claude to perform a self-review of the code changes before merging, preventing common bugs from leaking into master.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a senior code reviewer. Review the current git diff for potential race conditions or security vulnerabilities.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Review the current changes and suggest improvements before merging'

pro_tips: Always use the '--yes' flag in Claude Code only after verifying the diff via Antigravity to maintain human-in-the-loop control.
Alias your most complex Claude Code commands in your shell profile to save time during rapid prototyping cycles.
Use Antigravity to generate 'TODO' comments in your code, then trigger Claude Code to sweep and resolve them in one command.
Keep your context window clean by periodically clearing the Claude Code chat history for specific long-running features.
Configure a global git hook that triggers a Claude Code analysis whenever you attempt to push to the main branch.
content.next_steps: Once your environment is configured, focus on deploying your first project to a hosting provider like Vercel or Railway. Use the history of your commits to build a 'Changelog' file, which adds immense value to your portfolio documentation.
content.conversion_block: You now have the power to code without the fear of breaking your progress. This is the difference between a hobbyist and a professional engineer. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
